data.title: Apple gives trade-in discounts for new iPhones in China amid stiff competition
FTC probes Media Matters over Musk's X boycott claims - Reuters
data.description:
The U.S. Federal Trade Commission has requested documents from Media Matters regarding potential coordination with other media watchdogs allegedly involved in organizing advertiser boycotts of X, as claimed by Elon Musk, according to a document reviewed by Reuters on Thursday.

Elon Musk, who owns X, previously sued the liberal group over claims that it tried to damage his social media company’s relationship with advertisers.

The civil investigative demand seeks information about Media Matters' communications with other groups that evaluate misinformation and hate speech in news and social media, including a World Federation of Advertisers initiative called Global Alliance for Responsible Media, the report said.

X has ongoing lawsuits against both organizations.

The probe, first reported by Reuters, marks an escalation in U.S. government scrutiny of whether groups like Media Matters helped advertisers coordinate to pull ad dollars from X after Musk bought the social media site formerly known as Twitter in 2022.

Elon Musk disclosed in March that his generative artificial intelligence startup xAI has acquired his social network X in an all-stock deal.

Meanwhile, Ad agencies WPP (WPP) (OTCPK:WPPGF), Omnicom (OMC), Interpubliac Group (IPG), and Publicis (OTCQX:PUBGY) reportedly have agreed to set annual spending goals, or in talks to, with X in “upfront deals,” in which advertisers commit to buying slots in advance.

In other news, Musk, who has begun his transition away from serving as a key advisor to President Donald Trump in leading the Department of Government Efficiency, has committed to leading Tesla (TSLA) for the next five years.

data.title: UK retail sales rise 5% Y/Y in April
data.description:
• Retail sales in the UK increased 5% in April over the same month in the previous year. 

  • Retail sales jumped 1.2% M/M in April 2025, easily beating forecasts of a 0.2% rise and accelerating from a downwardly revised 0.1% gain in March.

  • This marks the sharpest monthly growth since January, driven by a 3.9% rebound in food store sales after declines in February and March.

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB)
  • Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: FTC withdraws lawsuit against Microsoft’s $69 billion Activision deal
data.description:
The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has ended its attempt to block Microsoft’s (NASDAQ:MSFT) $68.7 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard, withdrawing its complaint after losing an appeal to stop the deal.

This marks the conclusion of nearly two years of regulatory resistance, which began with the FTC’s opposition in 2023.

Microsoft’s president, Brad Smith, said on Thursday that the FTC’s decision to drop the case was “a victory for players across the country and for common sense in Washington DC”.

Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) cleared legal hurdles in July 2023 after a federal court denied the FTC’s injunction request.

data.title: Trump pushes EU to lower tariffs or face more levies - report
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump’s administration is pressing the European Union to unilaterally lower tariffs on American goods, warning that without such concessions, negotiations to avoid additional 20% “reciprocal” duties will stall, according to a Financial Times report.

data.title: Shell Indonesia to transfer gas stations operations to Citadel-Sefas venture
data.description:
Shell Indonesia, a wholly owned subsidiary of European oil and gas major Shell (NYSE:SHEL), has agreed to transfer ownership of its fuel filling station business in Indonesia. 

The buyer will be a new joint venture between Citadel Pacific and Sefas Group.

Shell's gas station business operations will continue as usual until the completion of the transfer of ownership, which is expected to occur next year.

Once the transfer of ownership is complete, the Shell (NYSE:SHEL) brand will remain in Indonesia through a brand licensing agreement, the company said in a statement.

Indonesia remains a key growth market for Shell’s lubricants business, where it operates a lubricants plant with a capacity of 300 million litres per annum and is building a grease manufacturing plant in Marunda that will have a capacity of 12 kilotonnes per annum.

data.title: Asian indexes climb as Treasury yields ease
data.description:
Asian indexes traded higher on Friday as U.S. Treasury yields eased after a rocky week due to worries in the bond market over mounting U.S. government debt.

Japan (NKY:IND) up 0.43% to 37,145, reversing losses from the previous session as investors reacted to the latest inflation report.

Japan’s core consumer price index, which excludes volatile fresh food prices but includes energy, rose 3.5% Y/Y in April 2025, up from 3.2% in March and slightly above market expectations of 3.4%. 

Japan’s 10-year government bond yield held steady around 1.56% on Friday, lingering near its highest level since 2008

China (SHCOMP) up 0.08% to around 3,383. recouping losses from the previous session as sentiment around Sino-US trade negotiations improved. 

The People’s Bank of China (PBoC) injected CNY 500 billion into financial institutions through a one-year medium-term lending facility (MLF) on May 23rd to maintain ample liquidity in the country's banking system. 

Beijing and Washington maintained high-level contact with a call on Thursday between U.S. Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau and China’s Executive Vice Foreign Minister Ma Zhaoxu, signaling ongoing communication following their recent trade truce. 

Hong Kong (HSI) climbed 0.58% to 23,679, swinging from the previous session’s drop.

Malaysia’s leading economic index edged down 0.04% month-on-month in March

India (SENSEX) up 0.69% to 81,506, partly recovering from the previous day's decline amid gains across almost all sectors, led by tech stocks.

Australia's (AS51) S&P/ASX 200 gained 0.14% t 8,360, recouping losses from the previous session, with financial and uranium stocks leading the rebound. 

Wall Street's major averages ended largely flat on Thursday, while a bruising bond sell-off from the previous session eased up. The focus remained on President Trump's "One Big Beautiful" tax bill, which was narrowly passed by the U.S. House.

U.S. stock futures traded in the red on Friday: Dow -0.04%; S&P 500 -0.04%; Nasdaq -0.09%.

data.title: Japan's core inflation rises to 3.5% in April
Xerox slashes dividend for the second time to $0.025, reiterates FY25 guidance
data.description:
• Xerox (NASDAQ:XRX) declares $0.025/share quarterly dividend, -80% decrease from prior dividend of $0.125. In the last quarter, the company had lowered the dividend by 50% to $0.125, implying 90% cut from Oct 2024 dividend of $0.25.
  • Forward yield 1.95%
  • Payable July 31; for shareholders of record June 30; ex-div June 30.
  • The board approved an update to its dividend policy in anticipation of the closing of the Lexmark transaction. In December 2024, Xerox announced a reduction to its dividend in conjunction with the planned acquisition of Lexmark, reflecting the prioritization of debt repayment following acquisition close. Since then, yields on Xerox publicly traded debt have risen, resulting in an increased cost of capital and placing greater value on the reduction of debt.
  • Xerox reiterates its 2025 guidance and continues to expect the Lexmark transaction to be de-levering upon transaction close and immediately accretive to adjusted earnings per share and free cash flow . The company continues to expect synergies associated with the Lexmark transaction of at least $238 million, realizable within two years.
  • The board also declared a quarterly dividend of $20.00 per share on the outstanding Xerox Holdings Series A Convertible Perpetual Preferred Stock. The dividend is payable on July 1, 2025, to shareholders of record on June 15, 2025. 
  • See XRX Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
9:35 AM Northwest Arkansas Fireside Chat 
 
Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis President Alberto Musalem and Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City President Jeffrey Schmid will participate in a discussion on the economy and monetary policy before the Northwest Arkansas Fireside Chat hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis.
 
10:00 AM New Home Sales 
 
New home sales measure the number of newly constructed homes with a committed sale during the month. 
 
Sales expected down to 700K rate in April from 724K in March, reflecting poor affordability and consumer caution.
 
12:00 PM Fed's Cook Speech 
 
Federal Reserve Board Governor Lisa Cook will speak on"Financial Stability" before virtual Seventh Annual Women in Macro Conference, co-sponsored by New York University's Graduate School of Arts and Science, New York University's Stern School of Business, and the University of Chicago's Becker Friedman Institute.
 
1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count 
 
The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs.
